summary: Phase 3 Files (folder create, multipart upload, share-links up to 30 days, public share redirect, PDF preview + Download lozenge, List/Grid view toggle) tested end-to-end. 7/7 backend pytest cases pass; all 4 frontend flows pass via Playwright against the public preview URL. One minor server bug found in days clamping (days=0 silently becomes 30 instead of 1).

minor.issue: When the client sends days=0, the response returns days=30 instead of the spec'd lower clamp of 1. Root cause: `days = int(body.get('days') or 30)` treats 0 as falsy and substitutes the default 30 before the max(1, ...) clamp runs. Negative ints (e.g. -5) DO clamp to 1 correctly because non-zero ints are truthy. Suggest: `raw = body.get('days'); days = int(raw if raw is not None else 30); days = max(1, min(days, 30))`.

verified_results.pytest_iter17: 7/7 PASSED in 7.30s (folder create + .keep hidden from files[], multipart upload + indexed in files_index + visible in tree, share-link POST days=30 returns app-URL token, share-link clamps days>30 to 30 and days<1 to 1 (negative path), share-link GET back-compat days=14, share/{token} 302 -> R2 with response-content-disposition=inline + 404 on bogus token, download attachment=false sets inline / attachment=true sets attachment)
verified_results.frontend_view_toggle: [data-testid=view-grid] swaps to files-grid layout; [data-testid=view-list] returns to files-tree + list rows. Persists in localStorage as 'filesViewMode' (verified by code review)
verified_results.frontend_new_folder: [data-testid=new-folder-btn] -> window.prompt('Playwright Test') -> POST /api/files/folder -> folder appears in BOTH list ([data-testid=folder-Playwright Test]) and grid ([data-testid=folder-grid-Playwright Test])
verified_results.frontend_upload: Clicked into new folder, set_input_files on [data-testid=upload-input], file pw_upload.pdf appeared in the file row listing within ~3s and was indexed into db.files_index (verified via subsequent GET tree)
verified_results.frontend_preview_modal: Click PDF row -> [data-testid=preview-modal] visible. After async dlUrl resolves, [data-testid=preview-download] (the yellow 'Download' lozenge) is rendered with href pointing at R2 and including response-content-disposition=attachment. [data-testid=preview-pdf] <object> tag also present.

action_items: (Optional, minor) Fix the days=0 clamp edge-case in files_routes.py::files_share_create — use `is None` check instead of `or 30`. Functionally low impact because the UI only offers 1/7/14/30 buttons.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /files/upload reads the entire upload into memory (await file.read()) before pushing to R2. Fine for the stated <50MB PDFs/photos/audio in Phase 3, but for larger franchise audio files (60-200MB) consider streaming via multipart UploadFile.read(chunk) and boto3 upload_fileobj to avoid worker memory spikes.

GET /files/share/{token} regenerates a fresh signed URL on every hit (good for 30-day links), but does not check object existence — if the underlying R2 object was deleted, users still get a 302 to a signed URL that 404s on click. Minor: head_object(rec['key']) before redirect to return a friendlier 410.
files_routes.py is now ~493 lines — still under the 700-line guideline. No action required.
Share token uses secrets.token_urlsafe(18) which is ~24 chars — sufficient entropy (>140 bits). Good.
test_credentials: admin@creativemojo.co.uk / CreativeMojo2026! — used from /app/memory/test_credentials.md successfully throughout.
seed_data_creation: All test artifacts (folder 'Playwright Test/.keep' + uploaded files under admin/test-iter17-<ts>/ and under 'Playwright Test/') were cleaned up via DELETE /api/files at the end of the run. Verified empty via subsequent tree query.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 Files functionality is fully working end-to-end. Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter17_files.py is the regression suite for: folder create, multipart upload, share-link (POST + GET back-compat), 30-day clamp, public share redirect (302 to R2 with response-content-disposition=inline), 404 on bad token, and download disposition inline/attachment. Note the one minor `days=0 -> 30` bug if you intend to add a 'custom days' input later. All UI testids in FilesPage.js are stable: files-page, files-tree, files-grid, view-list, view-grid, new-folder-btn, upload-btn, upload-input, preview-modal, preview-pdf, preview-download, preview-close, share-modal, share-days-{1|7|14|30}, share-url, share-close, folder-<name>, folder-grid-<name>, file-row-<key>, file-grid-<key>, preview-file-<key>, share-btn-<key>, download-grid-<key>.
